title: Ex situ conservation
script_summary: A dive into the science and uncertainty of ex situ conservation.

segments.voiceover: What if saving species means taking them away from their homes?
Ex situ conservation involves protecting species by moving them to controlled environments.
This method raises questions: Is it really effective? What are the risks?
Studies show mixed results; some species thrive, others struggle in captivity.
Ultimately, ex situ conservation can be a lifeline, but it’s not a guaranteed fix.
Curious about how we can better protect our species? Hit that follow button for more!
